metadata.title: Product Experiences
metadata.description: Outcome-based product experiences for the Bike Mobility domain, linking customer and business outcomes to reusable product bricks and required external systems.

definitions.productBrick: A reusable implementation-facing building block used to realize one or more product experiences.
definitions.productCapability: A high-level, outcome-based experience that delivers customer or business value.

metadata.flowModelNote: Each experience now carries only experience-specific flows. Each flow step lists the bricks and external systems used inside that experience.

capabilities.name: Launch a Bike Benefit Program
capabilities.description: Help an employer mobility manager set up a bike benefit program with the right policies, subsidy rules, and rollout controls.
outcomes: Policy setup feels controlled instead of bespoke

flows.title: Launch a Bike Benefit Program
flows.description: Help an employer mobility manager set up a bike benefit program with the right policies, subsidy rules, and rollout controls.

steps.title: Create employer program
keyFacts: This step determines how quickly users reach initial value.
It often combines setup effort, policy choices, and correctness checks.
painPoints: Users stall when setup asks for too much context too early.
Errors here create rework and reduce confidence in the product model.

dependencies.name: Employer Policy & Program Management
dependencies.description: Configures employer-level mobility programs including subsidy logic, plan design, market variations, approval rules, and policy governance.
dependencies.how: Uses Employer Policy & Program Management for employer-level mobility programs including subsidy logic, plan design, market variations, approval rules, and policy governance in create employer program.

dependencies.name: Quote, Subsidy & Savings Configurator
dependencies.description: Calculates rider and employer cost scenarios, subsidy rules, and savings projections before enrollment or policy changes.
dependencies.how: Uses Quote, Subsidy & Savings Configurator for rider and employer cost scenarios, subsidy rules, and savings projections before enrollment or policy changes in create employer program.

capabilities.name: Keep Program and Payroll in Sync
capabilities.description: Help employers keep eligibility, payroll deduction, and settlement outcomes aligned across employee lifecycle events.
outcomes: Less manual reconciliation for employer admins

flows.title: Keep Program and Payroll in Sync
flows.description: Help employers keep eligibility, payroll deduction, and settlement outcomes aligned across employee lifecycle events.

steps.title: Review employee eligibility
keyFacts: This step supports user visibility, decision quality, and operational control.
Users need concise signals, not raw system output.
painPoints: Users struggle when the state is hard to interpret or act on.
Decision quality drops when key signals are delayed or buried.

dependencies.name: Eligibility, Payroll & HRIS Sync
dependencies.description: Synchronizes employee eligibility, payroll deductions, employment events, and employer master data with HR and payroll systems.
dependencies.how: Uses Eligibility, Payroll & HRIS Sync for employee eligibility, payroll deductions, employment events, and employer master data with HR and payroll systems in review employee eligibility.

steps.title: Sync payroll deductions
keyFacts: This step converts intent into a real transaction or commitment.
Trust, clarity, and feedback matter more here than feature breadth.
painPoints: Users hesitate when they do not trust the outcome, price, or handoff.
Small friction at this point creates disproportionate conversion loss.

capabilities.name: Find the Right Bike
capabilities.description: Help a rider discover the right bike and package for their needs, employer policy, and budget.
outcomes: Selection feels informed rather than overwhelming

flows.description: Help a rider discover the right bike and package for their needs, employer policy, and budget.

steps.title: Browse bike catalog
keyFacts: This step shapes first relevance and perceived product quality.
Users decide quickly whether the marketplace understands their intent.
painPoints: Low relevance or weak filtering creates early abandonment.
Users lose trust when the result set feels noisy or poorly explained.
